hypothesis: If the root-mean-square difference between gradient-derived conservative forces and directly-predicted non-conservative forces (U∆) is used as a data-free uncertainty metric for direct-force pre-trained NNIPs, then U∆ will strongly correlate with true force error (εdirect) across diverse solids, surfaces, and molecules and will more effectively detect out-of-distribution/adversarial structures than within-family ensemble variance because disagreement between conservative and non-conservative force predictions signals model unreliability.
